passages.title: A survivor's story
passages.source_html:
A survivor's story


One native bird in New Zealand that has managed to survive the introduction of non-native species


A 


As an island country with a fauna dominated by birds, New Zealand was once home to an owl species which is now extinct, the 'laughing owl', named for its distinctive cry. This bird was widespread throughout the islands when European settlers arrived in the middle of the 19th century and it remained in good numbers for some years thereafter. Where other native birds suffered from predation by the Polynesian rat, the laughing owl turned the tables and adapted its diet to include the rodent. It was also capable of catching and killing the other New Zealand owl, the morepork, and even larger birds, such as the weka. However, the laughing owl was wiped out around the beginning of the 20th century. Its demise was caused by specimen collectors, habitat changes, and non-native predators including cats and stoats. Surprisingly, it is the smaller owl, the morepork, that has managed to survive until this day.


B


Speckled dark brown, with yellow eyes and long tails, they are around 29 centimeters long from head to tail and 175 grams in weight. Moreporks have fringes on the edge of their feathers, so they can fly almost silently and not alert potential prey. They have acute hearing and their large eyes are very sensitive to light.


C


Moreporks nest in tree hollows, in clumps of plants, or in cavities among rocks and roots, in the wild. Moreporks usually start nesting in October, although zoo specimens have been recorded nesting in midwinter, possibly stimulated by an ample food supply. The female lays up to three white eggs, which she incubates for 20 to 30 days. During this time, she rarely hunts, and the male brings food to her. Once the chicks hatch, she stays mainly on the nest until the young owls are fully feathered. When hatched, chicks are covered in light grey down, and have their eyes closed. The eyes do not open until the eighth day after hatching. They can fly at around 35 days.


D


By day, moreporks sleep in roosts. By night, they hunt a variety of animals – mainly large invertebrates including scarab and huhu beetles, moths, caterpillars and spiders. They also take small birds and mice. They can find suitable food in pine forests as well as native forest. A morepork uses its sharp talons to catch or stun its prey, which it then carries away in its bill. Moreporks are clever hunters, and birds such as robins, grey warblers and fantails can end up as their prey. In the day, these small birds sometimes mob drowsy moreporks and chase them away from their roosts. They force the sleepy predators to search for a more peaceful spot.


E


Moreporks have proved to be ungracious hosts. Scientists trying to establish a population of plovers on Motuora island in New Zealand's Hauraki Gulf were mystified as to why only two birds survived out of 75 placed there. The culprits turned out to be five pairs of moreporks that ate or chased away the new arrivals.


F 


Although moreporks are still considered to be relatively common, it is likely that numbers are in gradual decline due to predation and loss of habitat. As the female is a hole-nester, she is vulnerable to predators such as stoats and possums during the breeding season, and eggs and chicks will also be at risk from rats. The use of pesticides is another possible threat to the owls though not a direct one. As moreporks are at the top of the food chain, they could be affected by an accumulative poison by consuming prey that has ingested poison.


G


The New Zealand Department of Conservation is taking steps to ensure the preservation of New Zealand's only native owl. The department is involved in measuring the population of moreporks and has put transmitters on a number of birds to determine survival and mortality. As well as being New Zealand's only native owl, the morepork has symbolic and spiritual importance, so in monitoring the birds it is hoped that the morepork will continue to survive and thrive.


H


At dusk, the melancholy sound of the morepork can be heard in forests and parks as it calls to other moreporks and claims territory. Its Maori name (ruru) echoes its two-part cry. In the tradition of the Maori people of New Zealand, the morepork, or ruru, was often seen as a careful guardian. A number of sayings referred to the bird's alertness. As a bird of the night, it was associated with the spirit world. Moreporks were believed to act as messengers to the gods in the heavens, flying along spiritual paths in the sky. They were the mediums used to communicate with the gods. The occasional high, piercing call of the morepork signified bad news, but the lower-pitched and more common 'ruru' call heralded good news.

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1 - 7


Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, or choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.


1

Early European settlers made detailed studies of the morepork.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

审题：题目询问早期欧洲定居者是否对morepork进行了详细研究。


定位关键信息：European settlers，morepork，detailed studies


解题思路：文章中并没有提到早期欧洲定居者对morepork的详细研究。文章更多介绍的是laughing owl和morepork的生态特点和现存情况，并没有提到早期定居者的相关研究。


2

The Polynesian rat had a negative effect on the number of laughing owls.

正确答案： B. FALSE

审题：题目询问波利尼西亚鼠是否对笑鸮数量产生了负面影响。


定位关键信息："Polynesian rat"和“negative effect”


对应原文：A段，“Where other native birds suffered from predation by the Polynesian rat, the laughing owl turned the tables and adapted its diet to include the rodent.”


解题思路：原文指出波利尼西亚鼠对其他鸟类有负面影响，但笑鸮通过将波利尼西亚鼠纳入其食谱而适应，因此波利尼西亚鼠对笑鸮并无负面影响。


同义替换：题目中的“negative effect”替换为“suffered from predation”。


3

The laughing owl was larger than the morepork.

正确答案： A. TRUE

审题：题目询问笑鸮是否比morepork体型更大。

定位关键信息："laughing owl"（笑鸮）和"larger"（更大）。


对应原文：A段，“However, the laughing owl was wiped out around the beginning of the 20th century. Its demise was caused by specimen collectors, habitat changes, and non-native predators including cats and stoats. Surprisingly, it is the smaller owl, the morepork, that has managed to survive until this day.”


解题思路：原文中提到laughing owl在20世纪初灭绝，而体型更小的morepork生存至今。


4

Rats pose a risk to young moreporks.

正确答案： A. TRUE

审题：题目询问老鼠是否对幼年morepork构成威胁。


定位关键信息："rats"和“young morepork”


对应原文：F段，“As the female is a hole-nester, she is vulnerable to predators such as stoats and possums during the breeding season, and eggs and chicks will also be at risk from rats.”


解题思路：原文明确表示老鼠会对morepork的蛋和幼崽造成威胁。



5

The New Zealand Department of Conservation is hoping to limit the population of moreporks.

正确答案： B. FALSE

审题：题目询问新西兰保护部是否希望限制morepork的数量。


定位关键信息："New Zealand Department of Conservation"和"limit the population"


对应原文：G段，“The department is involved in measuring the population of moreporks... it is hoped that the morepork will continue to survive and thrive.”


解题思路：原文提到保护部希望morepork种群能够继续生存并繁衍，而不是限制其数量。

6

Other bird species are frightened away when they hear the morepork's cry.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

审题：题目询问是否有其他鸟类听到morepork的叫声后感到害怕而逃离。


定位关键信息："Other bird species"和"frightened away"


对应原文：没有明确提到其他鸟类因为morepork的叫声而逃走的内容。


解题思路：文章只提到morepork的捕猎策略和叫声在毛利文化中的象征意义，但没有说明其他鸟类听到叫声后的反应。


7

In Maori tradition, the low call of the morepork had negative associations.

正确答案： B. FALSE

审题：题目询问在毛利传统中，morepork的低音叫声是否具有负面意义。


定位关键信息："Maori tradition"、"low call"和“negative associations”


对应原文：H段，“The occasional high, piercing call of the morepork signified bad news, but the lower-pitched and more common 'ruru' call heralded good news.”


解题思路：原文提到morepork的高音叫声预示坏消息，而低音叫声预示好消息，因此低音叫声没有负面意义。


Questions 8 - 13


Complete the notes. Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER in each gap.

8

The Morepork

Appearance

      – approximately  in length

      – feathers with fringes to enable quiet flight

正确答案： 8 29 centimeters##29 centimetres
Question 8

审题：题目询问morepork的长度。


定位关键信息：关键词为"length"（长度）。


对应原文：B段，“they are around 29 centimeters long from head to tail。”


解题思路：原文直接描述了morepork的长度为29厘米。


同义替换：题目中的“in length”替换为“29 centimeters long”

9

Nesting

      – nests in trees, plants or spaces in roots and 

      – after about 35 days, baby moreporks are capable of leaving the nest

正确答案： 9 rocks
Question 9

审题：题目询问morepork的筑巢地点。


定位关键信息："nests"和"roots"


对应原文：C段，“Moreporks nest in tree hollows, in clumps of plants, or in cavities among rocks and roots.”


解题思路：原文明确列出了morepork的筑巢地点，包括“岩石和树根中的缝隙”。


同义替换：题目中的“spaces in roots”与“cavities among rocks and roots”同义。

10

Hunting

      – transports its prey using its 

正确答案： 10 bill
Question 10

审题：题目询问morepork用什么携带猎物。


定位关键信息："transports its prey"


对应原文：D段，“which it then carries away in its bill”。


解题思路：原文直接提到morepork用“嘴”来携带猎物。


同义替换：题目中的“transports”替换为“carries away”。


11

      – can be chased away by other birds during the

正确答案： 11 day
Question 11

审题：题目询问morepork在何时被其他鸟类驱逐。


定位关键信息："chased away by other birds"。


对应原文：D段，“In the day, these small birds sometimes mob drowsy moreporks and chase them away from their roosts.”


解题思路：原文指出morepork在白天被其他小鸟驱逐。


12

      – attacked that had been introduced to Motuora island

正确答案： 12 plovers
Question 12

审题：题目询问morepork攻击了什么被引入Motuora岛的生物。


定位关键信息：“attacked”和"Motuora island"


对应原文：E段，“Scientists trying to establish a population of plovers…The culprits turned out to be five pairs of moreporks。”


解题思路：原文提到morepork攻击了被引入的plovers（鸻鸟）。

13

Threats

      – may be exposed to in their prey

正确答案： 13 poison
Question 13

审题：题目询问morepork在猎物中可能接触到什么。


定位关键信息：“exposed to”和"prey"


对应原文：F段，“As moreporks are at the top of the food chain, they could be affected by an accumulative poison by consuming prey that has ingested poison.”


解题思路：原文明确提到morepork可能通过猎物接触到“累积性毒素”。
